violations.description: Personal information fields (name, DOB, phone, email) have autocomplete='off' instead of appropriate autocomplete tokens. WCAG 1.3.5 requires that common personal fields use proper autocomplete values to help users with cognitive disabilities fill forms automatically.
violations.reference_fix:
<input type="text" id="firstname" name="firstname" autocomplete="given-name">
<input type="text" id="lastname" name="lastname" autocomplete="family-name">
<input type="text" id="dob" name="dob" autocomplete="bday">
<input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone" autocomplete="tel">
<input type="email" id="email" name="email" autocomplete="email">

violations.description: The multi-step progress indicator (Step 1 of 3) is not marked up with appropriate ARIA attributes (e.g., role='progressbar' or aria-current='step') to convey progress state to screen readers.
violations.reference_fix: <div class="status-bar" role="progressbar" aria-valuenow="1" aria-valuemin="1" aria-valuemax="3" aria-label="Form progress: Step 1 of 3, Personal Information">

page_description: A medical patient intake form with multi-step progress indicator. Personal fields have autocomplete disabled instead of proper tokens, and the progress indicator lacks ARIA markup.
